question: What is the total DRM for a two-Location FG: the 1st Location contains an 8-1 and a squad and has +2 total Hindrance to the target; the 2nd Location has a CX squad and no Hindrance to the target?
Since Inherent Crews don’t take counter form, do vehicles share the Good Order Status of their manning unit?
expected_answer: An AFV with a Good Order inherent crew is itself Good Order. An AFV without one is not.

question: Is a Location ADJACENT to itself? I.e., if a rule permits some activity in an ADJACENT Location, and does not “specifically exclude” the unit’s current Location from that activity, is the current Location included by default?
expected_answer: Yes. Yes.

question: Four infantry units are in a Location that is effected by a 2KIA IFT result. The Random Selection DR is 6, 6, 5, 3. How many are eliminated?
The Random Selection DR is 6, 5, 5, 3. How many are eliminated?
The Random Selection DR is 6, 6, 5, 5. How many are eliminated?
Are dummy units in a concealed stack with non-dummies considered Eligible Targets when rolling for RS on a sniper attack?
expected_answer: Yes; all the Dummies would be considered one (combined) Eligible Target.

question: When dealing with hexes that have multiple cloaking counters and a RS is called for (e.g. Detection, Sniper, etc) do you RS between each cloaking counter and then RS from there, or do you simply RS between any/all units inside of the cloaking counters? What if one or more of the counters is a dummy?
expected_answer: A.9 and A14.23 will apply normally. Ignore the cloaking counters unless they are empty (in which case they count as their own (dummy) unit or as part of all Dummies in that Location).

question: Does an Encircled, Fanatic 10-2 Leader have a 10ML for MC/TC/etc.?
expected_answer: Yes.

question: When a Partisan squad (or a squad with underlined Morale) fails to Rally with a Commissar: A) Is it Casualty Reduced? B) Is it split in two HS? When a Partisan half-squad (or a HS with underlined Morale) fails to Rally with a Commissar: C) Is it Disrupted (for a non-partisan)? D) Is it eliminated?
expected_answer: A and D.

question: A HIP leader and a HIP squad are stacked together out of LOS of any enemy unit. The owning player wishes to deploy the HIP squad. Must the squad be placed on board before making the deployment DR?
expected_answer: Both the squad and the leader must be placed on board.

question: May a mortar spotter deploy? If so, does the mortar spotter designation deploy with one of the HS similar to a SW?
expected_answer: Yes to both.

question: Can a leader Deploy a Spotter? If yes, could the spotting duty be taken over by only one of the resulting HSs, freeing up the other HS for other purposes?
expected_answer: Yes. Yes. {1}

question: A Breach exists in an Interior Factory Wall in a game or scenario to which the Red Barricades and Red Factories rules apply. What is the US# of the unit currently wishing to cross the breached hexside in the case of a crew attempting to push a Gun which is not a large target through the Breach?
At what level are off-board hexes treated to be at?
expected_answer: For boards, those hexes are generally at level zero; some SSRs modify this.

question: A scenario is in play, and one side has a reinforcement group that is specified to enter 'on/after turn X'. The player sets up reinforcements off board to enter at the beginning of his turn X. Are these units required to enter on turn X as specified by A2.5?
expected_answer: Yes. See also ASOP step 1.11A: '...Set up, offboard, all forces due to enter in this Player Turn...' Don’t set them up if you do not want them to enter.

question: Must Vehicle units specified to enter a given hex from off board set up in a line (i.e., in hexes of the same row) or may they merge from adjacent hexrows?
expected_answer: No. They may set up in H & J and then merge.

question: If the units are required to enter via a single road hex, must they set up on the hypothetical off board road hexes, or may they merge from adjacent hexrows?
expected_answer: No. They may merge.

question: Do the above units have to enter paying the road movement rate, or may they enter paying open ground rates by merging from an adjacent hexrow?
expected_answer: No. They may pay non-road COT. {4}

question: If using part of a board with a hill divided by the onboard/offboard line, what level are the offboard hill hexes at?
expected_answer: Ordinarily, such hexes are at level 0, barring an SSR or special (e.g., HASL) rule.

question: During the movement phase, may offboard units perform movement-related actions (such as double time declaration, changing BU/CE status, VCA changes, stopping, starting, delaying, loading/unloading, abandoning and scrounging) as long as they enter mapboard at the end of the phase?
expected_answer: Double Time declaration: Yes. Changing BU/CE status: Yes. VCA changes: Yes. Stopping/Starting/Delaying: Yes. Loading/Unloading: No. Abandoning/Scrounging: No.

question: Are units in offboard hexes immune to Sniper attack as well as 'fire'?
expected_answer: Yes.

question: Are units in offboard Open Ground hexes subject to increased movement costs (such as E3.65, E3.724, or any other condition that increases the normal movement cost)?
expected_answer: Yes.

question: Are units in offboard Open Ground hexes subject to Bog or Immobilization rules for any reason?
expected_answer: No.

question: Can an offboard AFV attempt to increase its available MP via an Excessive Speed Breakdown DR?
Given a scenario with only board 41 in play, are hexes W1 & W10 stream hexes due to A2.6?
expected_answer: Moving to off the board from stream hex W1 (or W10) to the imaginary mirror image stream hex would be crossing a stream hexside at the same level -1. (A2.76).

question: Is there any order of applying terrain transformations? Should a dry stream be converted into a gully before connecting via A2.76/G.1 or after?
expected_answer: Dry stream is a gully, and gullies don’t connect.

question: May a unit fire at a Depression hex if they have LOS to the crest level but not into the Depression in the following situations: A) The hex is devoid of non-HIP units? B) The hex has a non-HIP unit IN the Depression, but no non-HIP unit at crest level?
expected_answer: Yes to both; only units at crest level would be affected (other than for WP).

question: If situation B is true, then would the following also be true: 1) Would a unit firing Smoke/WP be subject to the +2 Case K DRM to the TH DR? 2) If a hit is obtained with Smoke/WP, would it rise from the Depression level? 3) Would a WP hit cause the non-HIP unit IN the Depression to take a WP NMC?
expected_answer: Yes to all three.

question: Must a player set up all units/concealment/fortifications in his scenario given OB? If not, are units not set up considered eliminated for CVP and, if so, is it immediate or at scenario end?
expected_answer: No. No. Yes, they would be considered eliminated or captured at the option of the other player. Immediately. At the option of the other player.

question: Must a player set up all units, fortifications, and other counters listed in his OB? Do units not set up count as eliminated for CVP, and do unplaced counters count as captured/controlled for VC?
expected_answer: No. Eliminated, captured, or controlled at other player’s option. Yes. As soon as it might be material to the VC.

question: Must a player enter all units and counters (e.g., motorcycles/horses) scheduled to enter as reinforcements? If not, do the unentered ones count as eliminated for CVP and captured/controlled for VC?
expected_answer: No. Eliminated, captured, or controlled at other player’s option. Yes.

question: May Dummies set up in a Minefield hex?
expected_answer: Yes.

question: A2.9 defines an enemy stack as 'all units/SW/Guns/entrenchment-counters in a given Location'. Is it legal to temporarily remove informational counters (e.g., WA, TCA, building level counter) from the stack to check the top counter—even if inspection is not allowed?
expected_answer: It is legal to see under informational counters since they are not part of the stack even if inspection is not permitted.

question: In scenarios where one side enters from off-board, is the player setting up on board required to indicate the (V)CA of concealed vehicles, Guns and 5/8” dummies, or is that information kept in a side record until those units move or fire?
expected_answer: Concealed units must be placed on board with their correct CA; the information is not kept in a side record.

question: In pre-game, if one side uses rubble counters to block stairwells in a multi-story building, can they then set up eligible units in an upper level Location of that building?
expected_answer: Yes.

question: Should A2.9 have errata applied so that it reads 'A unit/weapon may not set up overstacked or in a LOCATION it could not enter during the normal course of play'?
expected_answer: We will take that under advisement.

question: B23.41 states that a vehicle needs to be CT, fully-tracked and BU to enter a building, yet A2.9 limits setup to accessible Locations. May a CT, fully-tracked vehicle set up in a building if CE?
expected_answer: Yes.

question: Many scenarios state that OB units may set up entrenched. Does this mean that only extra foxhole counters may be used, or can Trenches, AT-Ditches, and sangers also be placed?
expected_answer: Yes, only foxholes, even when the usual reference to B27.1 is missing.

question: May vehicles set up in bypass?
expected_answer: Yes.

question: May a Goliath (German Vehicle Note 93) set up at scenario start in a hex other than its possessor’s, assuming its possessor has LOS to the Goliath’s Location and it is within 16 hexes?
expected_answer: The Goliath does not need to set up in its controller’s hex.

question: May a leader transfer a SW in the Rally Phase and then attempt to rally one or more units?
expected_answer: No.

question: Given A20.5 stating that the captor unit becomes the Guard and may only be relieved under specific conditions, does this mean a unit could transfer a prisoner and still perform other RPh/APh activities (like transferring a weapon, rallying, deploying, or repairing) in the same phase?
expected_answer: Yes.
